steps.title: Find the sentence boundary
steps.content: The first clause completes the idea that extra heat increases how fast water turns to vapor. The next words begin a new complete sentence about what happens in a pressure cooker pot, so the blank needs sentence-ending punctuation.

steps.title: Reject run-ons
steps.content: B gives no punctuation between the two statements. C uses only a comma, creating a comma splice. D adds and with, which does not properly join the clauses. The remaining choice ends the first sentence and begins the contrasting one. The answer is A.
